content:
===BEGIN===
In a world where sickness was unknown, I was the first to fall ill. At first, confusion gripped me—I didn’t understand the weakness, the fever, the ache. No one had prepared me for this experience. My body, once a fortress, now felt fragile and unfamiliar. The people around me watched with a mix of curiosity and fear, witnessing something they had never seen.

As the days passed, I realized that this vulnerability brought clarity. It reminded me that even in strength, there is fragility. I began to appreciate health not as a given, but as a precious asset. This experience reshaped my view on leadership: empathy, patience, and care are as vital as strategy and strength. In sickness, I found a new kind of strength—one rooted in understanding the human condition, a lesson I carry back to the boardroom and beyond.
